name: Richard Williams
abstract: Richard Gene Williams (May 4, 1931 – November 4, 1985) was an American jazz trumpeter.

concerts.label: Benefit Concert for the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee
Kool Jazz Festival: Benny Carter and Illinois Jacquet

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Williams, a great trumpet player, and Richard Williams, a fantastic trumpet player. He had a lot of good jazz players in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: We had Richard Williams at one time. We’ve had a lot of wailing cats in that band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joseph Rene
about_quotes.evidence: Let's see what they call this boy again. Richard Williams, I think. That's a trombone player next year, right? Richard Williams, yeah. See, I wasn't in the

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Mingus
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ... I remember, Richard Williams and Rolf Ericson and Charlie Mariano and -- who's the tenor player? Hafer [INTERVIEWEE] Dick Haneford.

about_quotes.speaker: Vince DiMartino
about_quotes.evidence: the other guy that was in there, that played the bridge, that’s Richard Williams. He was in the original Thad Jones-Mel Lewis band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince DiMartino interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 8, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: It was Clark Terry and a trumpet player named Richard Williams.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
